acceptOwnership().details: The new owner accepts the ownership transfer.

params.escrowId: keccak256(slug, payer, amount, nonce, chainId) — generado off-chain

owner().details: Returns the address of the current owner.

pendingOwner().details: Returns the address of the pending owner.

refundExpired(bytes32).details: CEI pattern: estado → Refunded ANTES del safeTransfer.

renounceOwnership().details: Leaves the contract without owner. It will not be possible to call `onlyOwner` functions. Can only be called by the current owner. NOTE: Renouncing ownership will leave the contract without an owner, thereby disabling any functionality that is only available to the owner.

params.releaseToMarketplace: true → release al marketplace; false → refund al payer

transferOwnership(address).details: Starts the ownership transfer of the contract to a new account. Replaces the pending transfer if there is one. Can only be called by the current owner. Setting `newOwner` to the zero address is allowed; this can be used to cancel an initiated ownership transfer.

createEscrow(bytes32,string,address,uint.notice: Crea un escrow moviendo USDC del payer al contrato via ERC-3009.

disputeEscrow(bytes32).notice: Marca como Disputed. Resolución via resolveDispute().

emergencyRefund(bytes32).notice: NA-204: Escape hatch — si nadie actúa en 30 días, CUALQUIERA puede         refund al payer. Preserva propiedad trustless del diseño original.

refundEscrow(bytes32).notice: Operador devuelve USDC al payer (agente falló o cancelación).

refundExpired(bytes32).notice: NA-204: Solo payer original u operador pueden refund después de timeout.

releaseEscrow(bytes32).notice: Operador libera el escrow al Marketplace (agente completó).         Backend luego llama WasiAIMarketplace.recordInvocation().

releaseExpired(bytes32).notice: NA-204: Solo operador o marketplace pueden release después de timeout.         Previene race condition: antes cualquiera podía llamar y decidir el destino.

resolveDispute(bytes32,bool).notice: NA-208: Resuelve una disputa — solo el owner (no operador) puede resolver.         Separación de roles: operador puede abrir disputas, owner las cierra.
